marketContext.conflictAlert: CEASEFIRE SIGNALS — Middle East pivot in progress

conflictInsights.label: Defense backlogs are irrevocable
conflictInsights.desc: Multi-year RTX, LMT, NOC contracts signed during the conflict cannot be cancelled — backlog strength persists regardless of ceasefire outcome

conflictInsights.label: Oil direction is the key variable
conflictInsights.desc: WTI pulled back from $118 to ~$91 on ceasefire signals — a confirmed deal pushes oil to $70–75; a collapse returns it to $110+. Position sizing in energy should reflect this binary

conflictInsights.label: Gold floor established
conflictInsights.desc: Central banks accelerating gold purchases as geopolitical norms shift — structural buyer base independent of any single conflict outcome

conflictInsights.label: NATO mandate is permanent
conflictInsights.desc: European 2% GDP defense commitment is now treaty-level — defense supercycle is structural, not tactical, and survives any Middle East resolution

themes.label: Geopolitical pivot
themes.desc: Iran ceasefire signals de-escalating conflict — oil pulling back from $118 peak, but defense backlogs and gold floor are permanent shifts

themes.label: Defense supercycle
themes.desc: NATO 2% GDP mandate + locked-in multi-year backlogs make RTX, LMT, NOC structural compounders independent of any ceasefire

themes.label: Gold at all-time highs
themes.desc: ~$3,310/oz — central bank buying + geopolitical uncertainty providing a structural floor well above historical norms

themes.label: AI capex intact
themes.desc: NVDA, META, AMZN, GOOG reported strong Q1 — enterprise AI infrastructure spend is disconnected from geopolitical headlines

themes.label: Healthcare secular
themes.desc: GLP-1 + aging demographics = multi-decade demand runway for NVO, ISRG, LLY regardless of macro cycle

themes.label: Fed frozen
themes.desc: Fed held for 3rd meeting — 8-4 hawkish split; near-zero chance of cuts in 2026 as CPI stays sticky at 3.3%. Rate-cut thesis is on hold

themes.label: TSX resilience
themes.desc: Canadian equities outperforming on energy + financials strength; BoC on hold at 2.25% but domestic economy more insulated than US

themes.label: Oil uncertainty
themes.desc: Ceasefire scenario = oil to $70–75 (bearish for energy names); collapse scenario = back to $110+ (bullish). Position sizing matters more than direction

recommendations.name: Berkshire Hathaway B
recommendations.thesis: Sitting on $330B+ cash amid tariff volatility — Buffett deploys aggressively in corrections. Natural market hedge with zero dividend drag. Complements tech-heavy TFSA with value ballast.
risks: Succession uncertainty post-Buffett
Underperforms growth in bull markets
Insurance exposure to catastrophic weather events

recommendations.name: Visa
recommendations.thesis: Digital payments toll-road untouched by tariffs. Global cross-border volume growing as travel rebounds. Asset-light royalty model: $0.001 per transaction × billions of transactions = immense free cash flow.
risks: Regulatory interchange fee caps (EU, US)
Crypto payment rails as long-term substitute
DOJ antitrust scrutiny of card network duopoly

recommendations.name: Intuitive Surgical
recommendations.thesis: 7,000+ da Vinci robotic systems installed creates razor/blade recurring revenue. New Ion bronchoscopy expanding TAM. Secular shift to minimally invasive surgery = 20-year compounding runway.
risks: Premium valuation (55x+ P/E)
J&J Ottava robotic system entering market
Procedure volume tied to elective surgery cycles

recommendations.name: Novo Nordisk
recommendations.thesis: GLP-1 peer to your LLY at a cheaper valuation. Ozempic/Wegovy demand still exceeds supply. Oral semaglutide + next-gen CagriSema data due in 2026. Portfolio: LLY + NVO = dual GLP-1 coverage.
risks: CagriSema Phase 3 trial miss risk
LLY oral tirzepatide as direct competitor
US drug pricing reform compressing GLP-1 margins

recommendations.name: ARM Holdings
recommendations.thesis: CPU architecture royalty on every advanced chip. Powers all Apple silicon, Nvidia data center CPUs, and AI edge devices. Scales to $100B revenue with zero manufacturing risk — the ultimate AI leverage play.
risks: RISC-V open-source architecture gaining traction
Customer concentration (Apple, Qualcomm, Nvidia)
SoftBank overhang on share price

recommendations.name: Axon Enterprise
recommendations.thesis: AI law enforcement platform (Taser + cameras + Evidence.com SaaS). Government contracts = recession-proof revenue. International expansion accelerating amid global security concerns. Zero dividend = pure TFSA compounder.
risks: Rich valuation (80x+ P/E)
Political / regulatory backlash on police surveillance AI
Government contract concentration risk

recommendations.name: Costco Wholesale
recommendations.thesis: Membership model is recession-proof — members don't cancel during downturns, they shop more. International expansion accelerating. Tariff pressure minimal as private-label pricing power offsets costs.
risks: Trades at 50x+ P/E — expensive for a retailer
Slowing international store openings vs expectations
Tariff pass-through compressing gross margins

recommendations.name: RTX Corporation
recommendations.thesis: The Iran conflict locked in multi-year Patriot, F-35, and Raytheon munitions contracts that cannot be cancelled regardless of ceasefire outcome — backlog now extends past 2031. NATO 2% GDP commitment is the structural driver that makes RTX a decade-long compounder independent of any single conflict. Pratt & Whitney at maximum F-35 engine production capacity. Thesis is now more durable post-de-escalation, not less — NATO rearmament just shifted from crisis-driven to treaty-mandated.
risks: GTF engine recall costs still unresolved
Cost-plus contract margin pressure from inflation
Ceasefire could slow near-term order intake optically

recommendations.name: Enbridge
recommendations.thesis: Largest North American pipeline — 30% of US crude flows through Enbridge's network. The key thesis here is rate-regulation: Enbridge's revenue is set by the National Energy Board regardless of spot oil price, making it the safest energy income play in a binary oil environment. If ceasefire sends WTI to $70–75, throughput demand is unchanged; Enbridge still collects its toll. 6.8% dividend eligible for Canadian dividend tax credit (0% WHT in TFSA). 29 consecutive years of dividend growth. Own for income, not oil price direction.
risks: Line 5 Michigan legal challenge — potential forced shutdown
Rate regulation caps upside on revenue growth
High debt load (D/E > 1.5x) — rate-sensitive balance sheet

recommendations.name: Lockheed Martin
recommendations.thesis: The conflict accelerated F-35 and THAAD orders that are now locked into a $170B+ backlog — ceasefire signals don't cancel a single unit. Hellfire, JASSM, and JAGM missile restocking continues as allied governments are legally required to maintain inventory minimums under NATO treaty commitments. Lockheed is the sole Western 5th-generation stealth aircraft supplier: no competition, no substitution. Cost-plus contracts protect margins in any inflation environment. 2.8% dividend in RRSP = 0% WHT under Canada-US treaty.
risks: F-35 production delivery schedule slippage
Budget continuing resolution delays new contract awards
Defense spending cut scenario in future US administrations

recommendations.name: Northrop Grumman
recommendations.thesis: B-21 Raider is in full-rate production — the Pentagon's urgency to modernize long-range strike capability is a 20-year program that predates the Iran conflict and survives any ceasefire. Sole-source contractor on the Sentinel ICBM, Columbia-class submarine weapons systems, and Ground-Based Midcourse Defense: these are nuclear triad contracts where no foreign alternative exists. The de-escalation actually benefits NOC — less crisis noise, same backlog visibility to 2033, cleaner re-rating path.
risks: B-21 cost overruns — fixed-price contract absorbs overages
Single-customer concentration (DoD = ~85% of revenue)
Nuclear triad modernization is politically contentious target for budget cuts

recommendations.name: Suncor Energy
recommendations.thesis: Oil direction is now a binary — ceasefire confirmation likely sends WTI to $70–75, which compresses Suncor's margin but still covers its $3.50/share dividend and buyback program. Suncor's ~$45/bbl break-even is the lowest of any major oil sands producer: profitable at $65 oil. Trading at a steep discount to US oil majors on free cash flow yield regardless of oil price scenario. CAD-listed (TSX) = 0% withholding tax in TFSA. Has repurchased 25%+ of shares outstanding since 2020. Size this position relative to your conviction on oil staying above $75.
risks: WTI to $70–75 on ceasefire squeezes margins materially
Oil sands carbon tax escalation in Canada
Pipeline capacity constraints limit export optionality

recommendations.name: Huntington Ingalls Industries
recommendations.thesis: The US Navy's sole builder of nuclear-powered aircraft carriers and one of only two submarine constructors. The 2026 naval shipbuilding budget — the largest since the Cold War — was passed before any ceasefire discussion and funds a 355-ship fleet target that requires decades of production. The carrier group deployments triggered by the conflict have demonstrated capability gaps that will sustain procurement regardless of regional outcome. Backlogs extend to 2032. No foreign competition. Steady dividend grower with a uniquely captive customer.
risks: Shipbuilding labor shortages delaying delivery schedules
Cost overruns on carrier/submarine programs absorbed by HII
Defense budget sequestration risk

recommendations.name: MercadoLibre
recommendations.thesis: Latin America's Amazon + PayPal combined. 220M+ users, 50M+ credit card holders. Tariff war benefits regional champions vs global platforms. Brazil and Mexico economies more insulated from US-China tensions.
risks: LatAm currency devaluation (BRL, MXN) erodes USD returns
Regulatory risk in Brazil — fintech licensing constraints
Amazon and Shopee aggressively expanding in LatAm

recommendations.name: Walmart
recommendations.thesis: Walmart has become an ad-tech company: $4B+ advertising revenue growing 50% YoY. Flipkart + international. Tariff-resilient: US supply chain more diversified than Target or Amazon peers.
risks: China tariffs increase cost of goods for private-label
Ad-tech revenue tied to discretionary ad budgets
International losses (India Flipkart, China exit)

recommendations.name: Shopify
recommendations.thesis: Canadian fintech/commerce platform powering 10%+ of US e-commerce. AI tools (Sidekick) driving merchant GMV growth. Freed from logistics. Expanding into B2B commerce globally. Zero dividend = pure tax-free TFSA growth.
risks: Growth premium valuation — punished severely on any miss
Amazon and TikTok Shop competing for same merchants
Merchant GMV exposed to consumer spending slowdown

recommendations.name: Accenture
recommendations.thesis: Every Fortune 500 firm needs AI implementation. Accenture's AI practice growing 40%+ YoY — $3B+ annual revenue. Captures 5-15% of every enterprise AI budget as the execution layer regardless of which model wins.
risks: AI tools commoditize lower-end consulting work Accenture depends on
Enterprise IT budgets are cyclical — cut in recessions
Revenue concentration in large multi-year engagements

recommendations.name: iShares S&P/TSX Capped Energy ETF
recommendations.thesis: Diversified basket of Canadian oil producers and pipelines — CNQ, SU, CVE, TOU, MEG — with the lowest collective break-even cost structure of any oil ETF. The ceasefire scenario (oil to $70–75) still keeps all major holdings profitable and dividend-paying. The collapse scenario (oil back to $110+) delivers outsized upside. CAD-listed on TSX = 0% withholding tax in TFSA. Best way to hold binary oil exposure with diversified downside protection across five major producers.
risks: Single-sector concentration — falls hard if WTI drops to $70
No individual company MOAT — broad energy commodity exposure
Carbon tax escalation is a structural headwind for Canadian oil sands

recommendations.name: iShares MSCI India ETF
recommendations.thesis: India = China replacement manufacturing hub. Apple, Samsung shifting production. 1.4B population, median age 28, 7%+ GDP growth. Modi infrastructure boom. Demographics + manufacturing shift = generational opportunity.
risks: Single-country concentration — political or macro shock hits entire ETF
INR/CAD currency risk on repatriation
Elevated valuations post-2023 rally (Nifty 50 at 22x P/E)

recommendations.name: Vanguard European ETF
recommendations.thesis: European stocks at 40-year discount to US on P/E. Defense spending surge (NATO commitment). ECB cutting rates faster than Fed. Deep diversification away from your US concentration.
risks: EUR/CAD currency headwind if ECB cuts faster than expected
Russia-Ukraine conflict escalation risk
Slower structural earnings growth vs US tech-heavy market

recommendations.name: iShares MSCI Japan ETF
recommendations.thesis: Tokyo Stock Exchange governance reforms forcing buybacks + ROE improvement. Yen weakness creates value for CAD investors. Buffett's Japan trading house bets validated. Cheap valuations uncorrelated to US tariff drama.
risks: BOJ rate normalization strengthens yen — reduces CAD returns from yen-based assets
Single-country concentration
Export-oriented economy exposed to US tariff escalation

recommendations.name: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond
recommendations.thesis: The Fed is frozen at 3.50–3.75% with a hawkish board — the rate-cut thesis is delayed, not dead. TLT at 4.35% yield is now pure carry while you wait for the eventual pivot. The trade is asymmetric: if oil falls on a ceasefire deal, CPI drops toward 2.8% and the Fed is forced to cut, delivering 15–20% capital gain on top of your yield. Hold in RRSP to shelter interest income from full marginal taxation. Do not over-size — the 'Fed cuts in 2026' scenario is now a 2027 story if inflation stays sticky.
risks: Duration risk — 20yr bonds fall ~15% per 1% rise in rates
Fed staying higher for longer delays the capital gain payoff
US fiscal deficit puts structural upward pressure on long-end yields

recommendations.name: BMO Aggregate Bond ETF
recommendations.thesis: BoC is on hold at 2.25% — the active cut cycle has paused, but ZAG.TO still earns 3.2% yield with zero currency risk and 0% WHT in TFSA. Its core value is portfolio ballast: when equities sell off (bear case scenario — oil spike, credit event), ZAG.TO rises. The BoC cut cycle will resume when domestic growth softens further — GDP projected at 1.2% for 2026 — making this a patient carry trade with defensive optionality. Right-size it as insurance, not a rate-momentum bet.
risks: BoC on hold longer than expected delays capital appreciation
Low total return vs equity alternatives in a risk-on market
Rising Canadian sovereign debt concerns if fiscal deficit widens

recommendations.name: iShares Canadian REIT ETF
recommendations.thesis: Canadian REITs deeply oversold from rate cycle. As BoC cuts rates, cap rate compression lifts valuations. Canadian-listed = no WHT in TFSA. Diversified industrial, retail, and residential REIT exposure.
risks: Rate-sensitive — falls when BoC stays on hold longer than expected
Office and retail REITs face structural headwinds from remote work / e-commerce
Single-country concentration in Canadian real estate

recommendations.name: Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF
recommendations.thesis: Companies growing dividends 10+ consecutive years: Visa, Microsoft, J&J, UnitedHealth. Quality factor in disguise — these firms have durable moats. RRSP: 0% WHT. Outperforms in drawdowns. MER 0.06%.
risks: Dividend growers lag in momentum-driven bull markets
15% IRS withholding tax on dividends if held in TFSA instead of RRSP
Concentration in financials and industrials (~40% combined)
